zh.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas於大約1360年之後出生在英國威爾斯。她於大約1386年之前與John Ap Grond在威爾士結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas于大约1360年之后出生在英国威尔斯。她于大约1386年之前与John Ap Grond在威尔士结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: McNichol
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Niocaill, a patronymic from the Gaelic form of Nicholas . This is the usual spelling of the name in Scotland, while it usually ends with double l in Ireland.

variants.name: McNicholas
meanings: Irish: Gaelic patronymic from   Nicholas  . In Ireland this was the surname of a Gaelicized Norman family. Compare   McNichol  .

variants.name: Mastrocola
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Marcello, Vittorio, Carlo, Luigi, Nicola, Rocco, Aldo, Angelo, Armando, Assunta, Camillo, Domenico, Elio, Mario.
Italian (southern): from a title of respect composed of mastro ‘master craftsman’ + Còla, a short form of the personal name Nicola (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Loos
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Loose or Loosey.
North German: from a short form of Nikolaus, German form of Nicholas .
Flemish and Dutch: nickname from the adjective loos ‘cunning, artful, guileful’.

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

variants.name: Loosli
meanings: Swiss German: from a pet form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Laus
meanings: German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus, see Nicholas .
Walloon and Flemish: nickname from a Frenchified form of the Dutch adjective loos ‘cunning, artful, guileful’ (compare Loos ), or a cognate of 1 above.
Croatian and Serbian (Lauš): from the personal name Lauš, an old variant of Lajoš, a Slavicized form of Hungarian Lajos, an equivalent of Louis .

variants.name: Lais
meanings: German: perhaps a nickname from Middle High German līse ‘quiet’, or perhaps from a dialect short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ). This surname is found mainly in MN.

variants.name: Lause
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Jurgen.
North German: from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Lauser
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German:
from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

nameMeanings.name: Gruffydd
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

en.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas was born after about 1360, in Wales, United Kingdom. She married John Ap Grond before about 1386, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas est née après environ 1360, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé John Ap Grond avant environ 1386, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas wurde nach um 1360 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat vor um 1386 in Wales John Ap Grond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas è nata il dopo il circa 1360 nel Galles, Regno Unito. Lei sposato John Ap Grond prima del circa 1386, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Glamorgan, Wales: ウェールズグラモーガン
ja.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholasは約1360年以後にイギリスウェールズで生まれた。約1386年以前にウェールズでJohn Ap Grondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas(은)는 대략 1360년 이후 영국 웨일스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1386년 이전 웨일스에서 John Ap Grond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas nasceu após aproximadamente 1360, em Gales, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com John Ap Grond antes aproximadamente 1386, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Gruffydd Ap Nicholas родилась после около 1360 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Ap Grond перед около 1386 г. в местности Уэльс. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
